refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall minimize cycle time for the load→travel→dump cycle by optimizing load handling, travel efficiency, and dump operations.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is broad and high-level; decomposing it into sub-functions allows targeted design parameters and clearer verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure cycle time components (load, travel, dump) separately and verify combined p95 cycle time meets CT_target under duty cycle and site conditions.

candidate_dps: Optimized travel path planning and control
selected_dp.selected_dp: Automated load handling system; Optimized travel path planning and control; Efficient dump mechanism control

potential_sources: Load, travel, and dump operations are sequential but have minimal functional overlap
Interfaces between sub-functions are well-defined and limited
coupling_check.notes: The selected DPs map cleanly to the sub-FRs with minimal cross-dependencies, supporting modular design.

assumptions_risks: Assumes accurate sensor data and control feedback for automation
Risk of integration complexity if timing coordination is not managed
Safety criticality requires robust fail-safe mechanisms in each DP
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider adding explicit safety-related DPs or constraints in next decomposition level.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Physical components responsible for load handling, travel control, and dump mechanism

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle sends operational status and cycle time metrics to NGHE Solution

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives commands from NGHE Solution or Teleoperation modules to execute load, travel, and dump tasks

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Sends safety alerts to Safety Services module

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communication backbone for command, telemetry, and safety data exchange

module_containment_scores.notes: Vehicle module contains core physical operations; high containment, low diffusion.
NGHE Solution oversees coordination but does not directly implement physical operations.
Teleoperation interfaces with Vehicle but does not contain core cycle time functions.
Safety Services monitors alerts but does not implement cycle time functions.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Vehicle module is the primary owner of cycle time related physical operations, minimizing diffusion and enabling clear responsibility.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with NGHE Solution for coordination logic
Risk of diffusion if Teleoperation duplicates control logic
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Clarify boundaries between Vehicle and Teleoperation modules to avoid control logic duplication.

fr_dp_gate.reason: FR refinement is clear and sub-FRs are logically decomposed; selected DPs map well to sub-FRs with minimal coupling.

diffusion_risks: Possible duplication of control logic between Vehicle and Teleoperation modules
Coordination logic diffusion between NGHE Solution and Vehicle
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Vehicle module shows strong containment for physical operations; interfaces are well defined.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by measuring cycle time metrics from Vehicle telemetry and validating task execution via Tasking interface.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of physical cycle time functions to Vehicle module with well-defined external interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Define explicit coordination interface and responsibilities between Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.
Consider safety-related FRs and DPs in next decomposition level.

decision.justification: While the decomposition and assignments are mostly sound, risks related to control logic diffusion and safety considerations require refinement before proceeding.

revise.why: Clarify module boundaries and interfaces to reduce diffusion risks and improve ownership clarity.

assumptions: Vehicle module can implement all physical cycle time functions
Telemetry and Tasking interfaces are sufficient for coordination
open_risks: Control logic duplication between Vehicle and Teleoperation
Safety criticality not explicitly addressed in current FR/DP/IM
evidence_needed: Interface definitions and control flow diagrams between Vehicle and Teleoperation
Safety requirements traceability and impact on cycle time functions
